# effector-lexical

> Effector bindings for the Lexical text editor. Lexical listeners become effector events; editor state is mirrored into stores ($text, $state, $json, $editable); edits and commands go out through effects. The framework-agnostic core owns the editor; a thin React adapter injects it into LexicalComposerContext.

Key idea: bridge Lexical's imperative API (mutable editor + register* listeners + commands) to effector's declarative stores/events/effects, with no subscription leaks (the model owns the lifecycle via destroy()).

## Docs

- [What & why](https://olovyannikov.github.io/effector-lexical/guide/getting-started): the imperative↔declarative bridge and design overview.
- [Installation](https://olovyannikov.github.io/effector-lexical/guide/installation): peer deps and entry points (`.` core, `./react`).
- [Quick start](https://olovyannikov.github.io/effector-lexical/guide/quick-start): model + React, and headless usage.
- [Core API](https://olovyannikov.github.io/effector-lexical/api/core): createEditorModel and the EditorModel units.
- [React API](https://olovyannikov.github.io/effector-lexical/api/react): EditorProvider, useEditorModel, useEditorInstance.
- [Recipes](https://olovyannikov.github.io/effector-lexical/recipes): autosave, load/persist, read-only, counters, toolbar, mutations.
- [Examples](https://olovyannikov.github.io/effector-lexical/examples): full React app and headless snippet.

## Optional

- [Specification](https://github.com/Olovyannikov/effector-lexical/blob/main/SPEC.md): behavioural contracts and design decisions.
- [Full LLM text](https://olovyannikov.github.io/effector-lexical/llms-full.txt): condensed API + recipes inline.
